THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: (SEE SIGNED "CONSENT JUDGMENT")

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ANTHONY FERREIRO'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT. NO APPEARANCE, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE SETTLEMENT COMPLIES WITH THE REQUIREMENTS FOR PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENTS UNDER HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTION 25249.7. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE WARNING THAT IS REQUIRED BY THE SETTLEMENT COMPLIES WITH HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTIONS 25249.5-25249.14; THE AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES IS REASONABLE UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW; AND THE PENALTY AMOUNT IS REASONABLE BASED ON THE CRITERIA SET FORTH IN HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTION 25249.7(B)(2). ORDER AND CONSENT JUDGMENT SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-19-2021 Off Calendar

MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF ANTHONY FERREIRO's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment.

There are no appearances in this matter.

There being no appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Plaintiff's motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2).

Order and Consent Judgment signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported (302/EPS)
